# nginx-ingress/delete_tls_secret
#
# The TLS Secret backing the HTTPS Ingress is deleted mid-workflow — simulating
# an accidental `kubectl delete secret` or an automated cert-manager rotation
# that fails partway through. The Ingress resource remains but HTTPS traffic
# starts returning TLS handshake errors. The agent must re-create the Secret
# (or renew it) so HTTPS access is restored.
params:
  namespace:
    default: demo
    description: Namespace the TLS Secret lives in.
  secret_name:
    default: expired-tls-secret
    description: Name of the TLS Secret to delete.

deploy:
  on_probe_fail: error
  probe:
    - command: "kubectl -n {{params.namespace}} get secret {{params.secret_name}}"
      timeout_sec: 10
  apply:
    - command: "kubectl -n {{params.namespace}} delete secret {{params.secret_name}}"
      timeout_sec: 20
  verify:
    - command: >-
        /bin/sh -c "kubectl -n {{params.namespace}} get secret {{params.secret_name}}
        2>&1 | grep -q 'not found'"
      timeout_sec: 10

lift:
  on_probe_fail: skip
  probe:
    - command: >-
        /bin/sh -c "kubectl -n {{params.namespace}} get secret {{params.secret_name}}
        2>&1 | grep -q 'not found'"
      timeout_sec: 10
  apply:
    - command: >-
        /bin/sh -c "kubectl -n {{params.namespace}} create secret tls {{params.secret_name}}
        --cert=/tmp/expired-tls.crt --key=/tmp/expired-tls.key"
      timeout_sec: 20
  verify:
    - command: "kubectl -n {{params.namespace}} get secret {{params.secret_name}}"
      timeout_sec: 10
